title: AST SAR: Kuskokwim River [AK21000385]
summary: On 1/2/21 at approximately 0920 hours, Troopers in Bethel were informed by the Bethel Search and Rescue that they were responding to a report of two snowmachiners that had driven into a hole in the ice on the Kuskokwim River downstream of Tuluksak. The Bethel SAR reported local residents of Tuluksak witnessed the snowmachiners drive into open water at 1935 hours on 1/1/21. On 1/4/21 two snowmachines and one body have been removed from the Kuskokwim River at the spot the two were seen driving into the hole in the ice. Searchers are continuing to search for the second individual.

title: AST Homicide: Emmonak [AK21000361]
summary: At approximately 0325 hours on January 1, 2021, Alaska State Troopers received a report of a deceased male at a residence in Emmonak. Troopers from Emmonak responded and located 39-year-old Brian Agwiak of Emmonak, deceased in his residence. Investigators from the Alaska Bureau of Investigation and Forensic Scientists from the State of Alaska Crime Lab responded to the scene to investigate. Investigation revealed that 27-year-old Rudolf Waska of Emmonak, assaulted Brian Agwiak until he was unconscious and then continued to assault him further. Agwiak later died a short time after the assault. Results from the State Medical Examiner are still pending. Waska was arrested and arraigned on charges of Murder in the 2nd Degree and Assault in the 1st Degree. Waska is being held on a $100,000 cash
